title: North Pacific Research Board Enhancement Act
short_title: North Pacific Research Board Enhancement Act
plain_summary: This bill makes changes to the North Pacific Research Board, which gives money to scientists who study fish and ocean life around Alaska. The bill adds one more member to the board who must be an Alaska Native with experience using natural resources for food and materials. It also makes it easier for the board to spend money on running their office, especially when they get less funding than the year before. For the next five years, the board can spend as much as needed on administrative costs without the usual 15 percent limit.
last_action_text: Placed on Senate Legislative Calendar under General Orders. Calendar No. 189.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the North Pacific Research Board Enhancement Act.

sections.title: Section 2 North Pacific Research Board Enhancement
sections.text: This section changes how the North Pacific Research Board works by making several updates to an existing law from nineteen ninety eight. First, it requires that money for the board must be made available within fourteen days after each government budget year starts, instead of just saying the money shall be used for the board. Second, it adds a new member to the board who must represent Alaska Native people and have personal knowledge and direct experience with subsistence uses. This person will be chosen by the board itself and then officially appointed by the Secretary. Both this new member and another existing member will serve for three year terms and can be reappointed one time. Third, the section changes rules about administrative expenses. Currently, no more than fifteen percent of the money can be used for administrative costs like running the board and managing grants. The new rule keeps this fifteen percent limit but adds an exception. If the board gets less money in a year than it got the year before, the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration can raise the fifteen percent limit for that year. This flexibility is meant to help keep the board running, make sure as much money as possible still goes to research, and maintain high standards for managing grants. Finally, for four years starting when this law takes effect, the fifteen percent limit on administrative expenses will not apply at all.
